methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Cortical Labs is a Series A-stage AI & Software company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable set spanning 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private peers including Liquid AI, Field AI, and Twelve Labs—all operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using $11M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the subsequent stage, implies a $22M valuation. The peer-median method references three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M). The median of this set is $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B. This wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in unit economics and market positioning within the AI & Software frontier-tech space.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $19M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.0B. The 120x spread between low and high estimates reflects two competing signals: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while peer multiples suggest substantially higher market values for AI & Software Series A companies. Key uncertainties include product-market fit validation, capital efficiency relative to peer cohort, and whether Cortical Labs' technical differentiation justifies positioning at the higher or lower end of comparable valuations.
